DISCOVERY 612, NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANT MARK P. CAMPEDEL'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY IS MOOT AS RESPONSES HAVE BEEN SERVED. THE COURT WILL NOT DETERMINE THE ADEQUACY OF THOSE RESPONSES ON THIS MOTION. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE LATE RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $$550, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly for order to show cause regarding sanctions as to John Lytle, Esq.

Appearances:
ROBERT RESNER, Esq. on behalf of plaintiff Creditors Trade Association
JOHN LYTLE, Esq. on behalf of defendant Mark P. Campedel

Having heard Mr. Lytle's representation of Mr. Campedel, the court dissolved the order to show cause.

Off Calendar Mar-01-2006 Continued To Jul-10-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Mar-02-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant MARK P. CAMPEDEL's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery is moot as responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. The court finds that the late responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $$550, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
